title: Aureline capability-lifecycle and dependency-marker vocabulary
description: Boundary schema for the capability-lifecycle row, the dependency-marker record, and the capability-lifecycle audit-event record frozen in docs/adr/0011-capability-lifecycle-and-dependency-markers.md. Every capability rendered on a protected surface (settings row, bundle card, command palette entry, install review step, docs / help page, support / export row, claim-manifest entry, and future SDK / API surface) resolves to exactly one capability_lifecycle_row_record, exactly zero-or-more dependency_marker_record instances, and emits capability_lifecycle_audit_event_record entries on the capability_lifecycle audit stream. Raw provider tokens, raw policy-bundle bytes, and raw kill-switch material never cross the RPC boundary; this schema carries ids and typed vocabulary only. The eventual capability-lifecycle crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool boundary every non-owning surface reads. Adding a new lifecycle state, support class, release channel, freshness class, client scope, marker kind, reason code, effect-on-parent value, audit-event id, or denial reason is additive-minor and requires a capability_lifecycle_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

capability_lifecycle_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this capability-lifecycle vocabulary export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles / claim manifests.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

lifecycle_state.description: Frozen lifecycle-state vocabulary. Readiness progression is monotonic through labs -> preview -> beta -> stable <= lts_facing; deprecated is reachable from any of those states; retired is reachable from deprecated only; disabled_by_policy is orthogonal and may overlay any non-retired state. preview -> labs is admissible when announced on the audit stream. A generic 'available' / 'unavailable' chip is forbidden; every surface renders one of these tokens or renders an 'unresolved_axis' repair hook.

support_class.description: Frozen support-class vocabulary. Independent of lifecycle_state: a capability may be lifecycle_state=stable while support_class=operator_only_support.

release_channel.description: Frozen release-channel vocabulary. Independent of lifecycle_state: a capability may live on stable_channel while lifecycle_state is preview (stable channel carries it under the preview posture) or on preview_channel while lifecycle_state is stable.

freshness_class.description: Frozen freshness-class vocabulary. A surface whose canonical owner has not been contacted within its refresh window MUST NOT render authoritative_live; it renders warm_cached, degraded_cached, stale, or unverified as appropriate.

client_scope.description: Frozen client-scope vocabulary. A capability whose client_scope excludes the rendering surface MUST NOT render as available on that surface; at most it renders a tombstone with a typed reason.

marker_kind.description: Frozen dependency-marker kind vocabulary. Every live marker on a capability row renders its marker_kind, its effect_on_parent, and its disclosure_summary; a generic 'has caveats' badge collapse is forbidden.

effect_on_parent.description: Frozen effect-on-parent vocabulary. gates_entire_capability absorbs the parent into disabled_by_policy or retired regardless of declared state; the other values narrow the specified axis via the monotonic-meet rule.

reason_code.description: Frozen marker-reason vocabulary. provider_grant_narrowed and provider_connection_unhealthy reuse the grant-resolution-reason set frozen in ADR-0010 rather than mint parallel vocabulary; consumers cross-reference schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json.

denial_reason.description: Frozen denial-reason vocabulary for the capability_lifecycle audit stream. Denials fail closed; silent downgrade to a generic 'unavailable' chip is forbidden.

audit_event_id.description: Frozen audit-event ids on the capability_lifecycle audit stream. Events MUST NOT carry raw provider tokens, raw policy-bundle bytes, or raw kill-switch material.

redaction_class.description: Declared redaction class. Mirrors the vocabulary used by ADR-0007, ADR-0008, and ADR-0010; the broker-owned redaction pass runs before bytes reach any persistent or exportable sink.

trust_state.description: ADR-0001 workspace-trust state at the moment the row / marker / event was recorded.

execution_context_id.description: ADR-0009 execution-context id when the row / marker was recorded inside a live execution. Null when the record was minted outside an execution context.

policy_context.description: Policy context the record was minted under. ADR-0008 / ADR-0009 policy epoch and ADR-0001 trust state are re-validated on every render.

earliest_removal_at.description: Earliest date the capability may transition to retired. Null when the removal horizon is open.

committed_removal_at.description: Firm removal date if committed. Null when the removal is announced but not yet committed.

deprecation_window.description: Deprecation window a deprecated row is obliged to render. A deprecated row without an announced_at is non-conforming.

display_label.description: Short human-legible label the surface may render next to the chip.

repair_hook_ref.description: Typed repair hook for a dependency marker or a disabled_by_policy lifecycle row. A marker or disabled row without a repair_hook_ref is non-conforming.

dependency_marker_summary.description: Light-weight inline summary embedded on a capability_lifecycle_row_record. Full marker payloads live in separate dependency_marker_record instances.

capability_kind.description: Which kind of capability this row describes. Settings, bundles, commands, provider-linked features, and the reserved SDK / API surface are the canonical six; workspace, extension, docs, and support surfaces round out the set.

capability_id.description: Stable id of the capability within its kind (setting id, bundle id, command id, feature id, capability id, SDK binding id, docs page id, support row id).

capability_ref.description: Reference to the capability this row describes.

effective_lifecycle_state.description: Render-time monotonic meet of declared_lifecycle_state and every live dependency marker's contribution. Every surface MUST render this value rather than declared_lifecycle_state when they differ.

effective_support_class.description: Render-time narrowed support_class when a marker narrows it. Null when no marker narrows support_class.

effective_release_channel.description: Render-time narrowed release_channel when a marker narrows it. Null when no marker narrows release_channel.

client_scopes.description: Client classes this capability surfaces on. A single-surface capability names one entry; a multi-surface capability names all applicable entries. A capability whose client_scopes excludes the rendering surface MUST NOT render as available there.

effective_client_scopes.description: Render-time client-scope set after marker narrowing. Empty when a client_scope_restricted_dependency has removed the surface entirely.

lifecycle_label.description: Renderer hint surfaces use when they project this row. ADR-0008 settings rows carry this field on effective_setting_record; other surfaces render it next to the primary chip.

deprecation_window.description: Required when declared_lifecycle_state or effective_lifecycle_state is deprecated. Null otherwise.

replacement_ref.description: Reference to the replacement capability when the row is deprecated or retired. Null only when the retirement is outright (no replacement).

since_label.description: Channel / milestone where the capability reached its declared state. Surfaced on docs / help pages.

live_dependency_markers.description: Inline summary list of live dependency markers. Full payloads live in separate dependency_marker_record instances with matching dependency_marker_id values. A surface rendering a capability row MUST also render every entry in this list.

disabled_by_policy_reason.description: Typed reason code when declared_lifecycle_state or effective_lifecycle_state is disabled_by_policy. Values come from the marker reason_code vocabulary (dependency_disabled_by_policy, dependency_kill_switch_tripped, managed_only_channel_required, client_scope_excludes_surface, freshness_floor_unmet) or the lifecycle denial_reason vocabulary. Null otherwise.

kill_switch_state.description: State of any kill switch attached to this capability row. armed means the kill switch exists but has not fired; tripped means it has fired and the row's effective_lifecycle_state MUST reflect the absorption.

dependency_marker_schema_version.description: Integer schema version pinned into the marker so a replayed marker can be validated against the exact shape it was minted under.

parent_capability_ref.description: Id of the parent capability_lifecycle_row this marker is attached to.

dependency_capability_ref.description: Id of the sub-capability this marker describes. For marker_kind = provider_linked_dependency this carries the connected_provider_record id from ADR-0010; for marker_kind = kill_switch_dependency this carries the kill-switch id.

dependency_lifecycle_state.description: Sub-capability's current lifecycle state. For provider_linked_dependency this encodes the effective grant-resolution posture (allowed_with_downgrade maps to preview-equivalent; denied_* absorbs to disabled_by_policy).

disclosure_summary.description: Human-legible paragraph naming the sub-capability, the reduced axis, and the user-visible consequence. A marker without a disclosure_summary is non-conforming.

refreshed_at.description: Timestamp of the last freshness refresh. A marker that crosses its freshness threshold without refreshing emits dependency_marker_stale_not_refreshed.

cleared_at.description: Populated when the marker is cleared because the dependency resolved back to the parent's declared state.

dependency_marker_id.description: Id of the dependency marker the event is about. Null for row-level events that do not reference a marker.

previous_value.description: Previous value on the affected axis (lifecycle_state, support_class, release_channel, freshness_class, client_scope) when the event is a change.

next_value.description: Next value on the affected axis when the event is a change.

denial_reason.description: Typed denial reason when the event records a denial. Null otherwise.

observer_subject.description: Stable subject reference for the observer that recorded the event (surface id, registry process id, claim propagator id). Never a raw token.
